title: Model Lab UI Simplification, Relative Development Signal Bands, and Nonblocking Map Cleanup

removed_map_blocking_ui.old_behavior: A floating Model Lab pill/card appeared over the SceneView and repeated overlay status.
removed_map_blocking_ui.new_behavior: Model Lab status is shown in the left Model Lab Controls and right Model Lab Intelligence panels only.

relative_signal_rank_band_wording.plain_english_description: Relative model research signal based on historical new construction patterns.
relative_signal_rank_band_wording.source_note: The existing safe preview source exposes top 1%, top 5%, top 15%, remaining, and insufficient-data bands. The UI uses Top 10-15% rather than inventing a precise top 10% split.

legend_color_updates.map_symbol_changes: Marker and aggregate surface profiles now derive presentation from relative band labels and use smaller halos to reduce visual clutter.

right_panel_simplification.technical_metrics_moved: PR-AUC, Lift@top 5%, and Precision@top 5% are hidden behind the Model QA details accordion.

driver_explanations.Zoning context: Historical/current zoning pattern was useful in the model.
driver_explanations.Transportation accessibility: Road, STIP, and AADT context helped separate stronger development patterns.
driver_explanations.Tax/value enrichment: Land, building, and value context improved the best internal model.
driver_explanations.New construction history: Historical permit outcomes were used as the model learning target.

selected_marker_context_changes.caveat: Internal research only. Not an official parcel score. Exact probabilities are not shown.

model_qa_details_behavior.safety_note: These are aggregate test metrics, not parcel-level scores.

report_includes: what the signal does not mean
snapshot_report_integration.required_caveat_verified: This is an internal research preview. It does not show exact parcel development probability and is not an official parcel score.

safety_verification.known_internal_terms: Safety scan still finds guardrail/schema/test terms such as prediction_probability_available, exact_probability_available, no_raw_model_scores, and internal development_signal_class usage in backend mapping/tests. The UI and preview response continue to use safe labels and false exposure flags.

dev_stack.frontend: http://localhost:3000 responded with 200 and was owned by the CFS Next dev process.
dev_stack.backend: http://127.0.0.1:8000 was owned by the CFS Uvicorn process.

browser_console: Existing opaque ArcGIS-style error message: r

remaining_limitations: The safe preview source currently exposes top 1%, top 5%, top 15%, remaining, and insufficient-data bands. The UI labels the moderate-high band as Top 10-15% rather than claiming a precise top 10% band.

A blind smoke-test click in the map did not conclusively select a research marker, though the SceneView hit-test path for model research preview markers remains implemented and the overlay reported visible safe research context.
